name: Pyfolio (Portfolio and Risk Analytics)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache-2.0); pyfolio-reloaded fork by Stefan Jansen

auth.notes: Local analytics library; no auth required

performance.notes: Matplotlib-based tearsheet generation; performance depends on data size

agent_readiness.notes: Portfolio analytics and performance tearsheets. pyfolio.create_full_tear_sheet(returns, benchmark_rets=benchmark). Metrics: Sharpe, Sortino, Calmar, max drawdown, rolling beta, rolling Sharpe, underwater plot. Factor analysis via Alphalens integration. Accepts pandas Series of daily returns. Commonly used with Zipline backtest output. pyfolio.create_returns_tear_sheet() for simpler output. empyrical library for standalone risk metrics.

gotchas: Original Quantopian pyfolio unmaintained; use pyfolio-reloaded
Matplotlib rendering can be slow for large date ranges
Some tearsheet functions require benchmark returns
Jupyter notebook output works best; script output harder
best_when: Generating backtest performance tearsheets (Sharpe, drawdown, rolling stats)
Risk metrics visualization after Zipline backtest
avoid_when: Production risk monitoring (use empyrical for programmatic access)
summary: Pyfolio (pyfolio-reloaded fork) generates portfolio performance tearsheets: Sharpe, Sortino, Calmar ratios, max drawdown, rolling beta, underwater plots. pyfolio.create_full_tear_sheet(returns). Accepts pandas Series of daily returns from Zipline or any backtester. Jupyter-native. Pairs well with Zipline and empyrical for complete quant research pipeline.
